message: chore(beads): close bd-5t6zo (printf family INT_MAX overflow)

 {"id":"bd-5ky","title":"Harness: Regression report for strict vs hardened (latency/risk Pareto)","description":"Create a report that summarizes tradeoffs.

Contents:
- Strict vs hardened deltas in latency (p50/p95) and risk/repair rates.
- Pareto cumulative regret trends.
- Any FDR/alpha-investing and approachability signals.

Goal:
- Make it obvious when a change improves safety but violates latency budget, or vice versa.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"VioletMeadow","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:42.328425057Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T18:02:08.192491089Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T18:02:08.192472995Z","close_reason":"Added strict-vs-hardened runtime_math regression report (per-mode subprocess metrics + Markdown/JSON output)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5ky","depends_on_id":"bd-2ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5ky","depends_on_id":"bd-3aa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-5rs1","title":"syscall: fsmount invalid fsfd should preserve unprivileged EPERM host contract","description":"On the current host, unprivileged fsmount(-1, 0, 0) and fsmount(-1, 1, 0) fail with EPERM rather than EBADF/EINVAL. Add focused ABI parity coverage so this privilege-first behavior stays explicit.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:49:55.553436878Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T21:56:26.613206683Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T21:56:26.611053276Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-5t03","title":"syscall: pidfd_send_signal invalid pidfd should return EBADF exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EBADF for pidfd_send_signal(-1, 0, NULL, 0). Add focused ABI regression coverage so the invalid-pidfd contract for this veneer is frozen exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:49:29.140778280Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:52:31.194324154Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:52:31.192191901Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity pidfd_send_signal(-1, 0, NULL, 0) behavior: exact EBADF and new ABI regression coverage; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-5t6zo","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] stdio printf family silently truncates return when total_len > INT_MAX","description":"crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs has 20 sites where snprintf/vsnprintf/sprintf/vsprintf and friends return total_len via 'as c_int' cast, truncating when the formatted-output length exceeds INT_MAX. POSIX requires EOVERFLOW + return -1 in that case (C11 7.21.6.5 p2 and POSIX.1-2024 snprintf). Concrete reach: a caller passing printf("%*d", INT_MAX, 42) or nested format specifiers that sum past 2^31 gets silent truncation of return value; downstream allocators that size buffers from the returned count are then under-sized and corruptable. Sites: lines 3284, 3354, 3404, 3484, 3499, 3549, 3627, 3642, 3674, 3727 (and 10 more). Fix: introduce printf_result_to_c_int(total_len) helper that returns -1 + sets errno to EOVERFLOW when total_len > c_int::MAX as usize, else total_len as c_int, and replace all 20 sites.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:40:45.030206230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:40:45.030206230Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-5t6zo","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] stdio printf family silently truncates return when total_len > INT_MAX","description":"crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs has 20 sites where snprintf/vsnprintf/sprintf/vsprintf and friends return total_len via 'as c_int' cast, truncating when the formatted-output length exceeds INT_MAX. POSIX requires EOVERFLOW + return -1 in that case (C11 7.21.6.5 p2 and POSIX.1-2024 snprintf). Concrete reach: a caller passing printf("%*d", INT_MAX, 42) or nested format specifiers that sum past 2^31 gets silent truncation of return value; downstream allocators that size buffers from the returned count are then under-sized and corruptable. Sites: lines 3284, 3354, 3404, 3484, 3499, 3549, 3627, 3642, 3674, 3727 (and 10 more). Fix: introduce printf_result_to_c_int(total_len) helper that returns -1 + sets errno to EOVERFLOW when total_len > c_int::MAX as usize, else total_len as c_int, and replace all 20 sites.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:40:45.030206230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:45:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"closed_at":"2026-04-23T23:45:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Fixed in b00ce291: printf_result_to_c_int() helper returns -1 + EOVERFLOW when total_len overflows c_int; all 20 sites in snprintf/vsnprintf/sprintf/vsprintf family now use it. 102 stdio_abi integration tests still pass."}
 {"id":"bd-5vr","title":"Epic: Runtime Math Kernels - 70+ Controllers","description":"70+ runtime math modules: risk.rs (conformal bounds), bandit.rs (constrained MAB), control.rs (primal-dual), barrier.rs (SOS certificates), cohomology.rs (sheaf overlap), hji_reachability.rs (HJI safety), mean_field_game.rs (Lasry-Lions), schrodinger_bridge.rs (entropic OT), rough_path.rs (Lyons signatures), persistence.rs (persistent homology), large_deviations.rs (Cramer), eprocess.rs (anytime-valid), cvar.rs (tail-risk), alpha_investing.rs (FDR), spectral_monitor.rs (phase transitions), clifford.rs (SIMD), 50+ more. Branch-diversity constraint: >=3 families/milestone, no family >40%.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:58:32.607246126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T22:48:32.050030273Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T22:48:32.049815517Z","close_reason":"10/11 children closed. All runtime math kernels implemented (786 tests across 71 modules). Statistical, algebraic, topological, control, optimization kernels complete. Formal proofs complete. Reverse-round methodology complete. Only bd-10pq (aarch64 arch heterogeneity) remains as future work.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["controllers","epic","frankenlibc","kernels","math"],"comments":[{"id":1032,"issue_id":"bd-5vr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:03Z"},{"id":1033,"issue_id":"bd-5vr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Card 1 dependencies acknowledged: runtime-math controller interactions must pass interference microbench and coverage drift guards.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:28:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5vr.1","title":"Math: Core kernel framework - trait interface, snapshot integration, evidence emission","description":"Shared framework for all 70+ runtime math kernels: common trait interface (evaluate, calibrate, snapshot), integration with RuntimeKernelSnapshot (154 fields), evidence emission contract (every decision emits structured JSON), offline synthesis -> runtime compact guard pattern. Must support branch-diversity constraint enforcement.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BoldFox","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:47.096325955Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T07:13:25.464524958Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T07:06:16.171430540Z","close_reason":"Completed framework contract expansion: reverse-round diversity snapshot + JSONL export APIs with tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["framework","frankenlibc","math"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5vr.1","depends_on_id":"bd-5vr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1034,"issue_id":"bd-5vr.1","author":"ProudBadger","text":"Progress update (bd-5vr.1): implemented a shared runtime-kernel framework contract in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs with explicit evaluate/calibrate/snapshot semantics plus evidence-export contract hooks.

Changes:
- Added RuntimeEvidenceContractSnapshot struct (seqno/loss/epoch counters).
- Added RuntimeKernelFramework trait with methods: evaluate, calibrate, snapshot, evidence_contract_snapshot, export_decision_cards_json.
- Added RuntimeMathKernel inherent API: evidence_contract_snapshot() and export_decision_cards_json().
- Implemented RuntimeKernelFramework for RuntimeMathKernel (delegating to existing decide/observe_validation_result/snapshot paths).
- Added focused tests:
  - runtime_kernel_framework_roundtrip_exposes_snapshot_and_contract
  - runtime_kernel_framework_exports_structured_decision_card_json

Validation:
- cargo fmt --all --check (PASS)
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_kernel_framework -- --nocapture (PASS: 2 tests)","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:39:28Z"},{"id":1035,"issue_id":"bd-5vr.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (CloudyLynx): expanded runtime-kernel framework contract in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs.\n\nWhat I added:\n- New reverse-round branch-diversity contract types: RuntimeReverseRoundDiversityState + RuntimeReverseRoundDiversitySnapshot.\n- New RuntimeKernelFramework methods:\n  - reverse_round_diversity_snapshot()\n  - export_runtime_math_log_jsonl(mode, bead_id, run_id)\n- RuntimeMathKernel implementation for both methods; factored reverse-round diversity computation into reverse_round_diversity_snapshot_from_cards().\n- export_runtime_math_log_jsonl now consumes the explicit diversity snapshot contract for coverage/diversity event emission.\n- Added framework tests:\n  - runtime_kernel_framework_exposes_reverse_round_diversity_snapshot\n  - runtime_kernel_framework_exports_runtime_math_jsonl_logs\n\nValidation run (all via rch exec):\n- rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --all-targets (PASS)\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_kernel_framework -- --nocapture (PASS, 4 tests)\n- rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-membrane --all-targets -- -D warnings (PASS)\n- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets (PASS; existing warnings outside this change remain)\n- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (FAIL due pre-existing unrelated warnings/errors in frankenlibc-core and frankenlibc-membrane tests, not introduced by this patch).\n\nLocal file-level formatting check:\n- rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs (PASS)","created_at":"2026-02-25T07:05:36Z"},{"id":1036,"issue_id":"bd-5vr.1","author":"BoldFox","text":"Claimed by BoldFox. Implemented runtime-policy export bridge in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs: added wrappers runtime_kernel_snapshot, runtime_evidence_contract_snapshot, export_runtime_decision_cards_json, export_runtime_math_log_jsonl; plus kernel_with_retry backoff for non-hotpath export calls and wrapper tests. Validation attempts were offloaded via rch. Current workspace has concurrent unrelated failures blocking clean gate completion (e.g. frankenlibc-core/src/string/regex.rs Ast::Repeat greedy field mismatch; clippy warnings in core/abi). rustfmt --edition 2024 --check on runtime_policy.rs passes.","created_at":"2026-02-25T07:13:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5vr.2","title":"Math: Safety kernels - SOS barrier, HJI reachability, Galois connection","description":"Implement safety-critical kernels: sos_barrier.rs (O(d^2) quadratic form evaluation, <30ns), hji_reachability.rs (HJI differential game safety certificates), sos_invariant.rs (Lyapunov synthesis), barrier.rs (certificate evaluation). Certificates compiled as &'static const data with SHA-256 proof hashes.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
